title: [HWMemSimImpl] Avoid generation of 0 bit Value
body:
`HWMemSimImpl` generates a zero bit result from `comb.extract` when the memory data is 1 bit width.
For example,
```
hw.module.generated @ram_2x1, @FIRRTLMem(in %R0_addr : i1, in %R0_en : i1, in %R0_clk : i1, out R0_data : i1, in %W0_addr : i1, in %W0_en : i1, in %W0_clk : i1, in %W0_data : i1) attributes {depth = 2 : i64, emit.fragments = [@RANDOM_INIT_REG_FRAGMENT, @RANDOM_INIT_MEM_FRAGMENT, @RANDOM_INIT_FRAGMENT], initFilename = "", initIsBinary = false, initIsInline = false, maskGran = 1 : ui32, numReadPorts = 1 : ui32, numReadWritePorts = 0 : ui32, numWritePorts = 1 : ui32, readLatency = 0 : ui32, readUnderWrite = 0 : i32, width = 1 : ui32, writeClockIDs = [0 : i32], writeLatency = 1 : ui32, writeUnderWrite = 1 : i32}
```
generates the following extract  of i0
```
           sv.for %j = %c0_i6 to %c-32_i6 step %c-32_i6_0 : i6 {
              %RANDOM = sv.macro.ref.expr.se @RANDOM() : () -> i32
              %7 = comb.extract %j from 0 : (i6) -> i0
              %8 = sv.indexed_part_select_inout %_RANDOM_MEM[%7 : 32] : !hw.inout<i32>, i0
              sv.bpassign %8, %RANDOM : i32
            }
```

This is a simple fix that creates a `c0_ui1` instead of the `comb.extract`: 
```
    sv.for %j = %c0_i6 to %c-32_i6 step %c-32_i6_0 : i6 {
              %RANDOM = sv.macro.ref.expr.se @RANDOM() : () -> i32
              %false = hw.constant false
              %7 = sv.indexed_part_select_inout %_RANDOM_MEM[%false : 32] : !hw.inout<i32>, i1
              sv.bpassign %7, %RANDOM : i32
            }
```
Related to: https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/8351
